1: And is moved out of his place.
2: And the sound that that goeth out of his mouth.
3: And his lightning unto the ends of the earth.
4: And he will not stay them when his voice is heard.
5: Great things doeth he, which we cannot comprehend.
6: And to the great rain of his strength.
7: That all men may know his work.
9: And cold out of the north.
10: And the breadth of the waters is straitened.
12: Upon the face of the world in the earth.
13: He causeth it to come, whether for correction, or for his land, or for mercy.
14: Stand still, and consider the wondrous works of God.
15: And caused the light of his cloud to shine?
16: The wondrous works of him which is perfect in knowledge?
17: When he quieteth the earth by the south wind? wind?
18: Which is Which is strong, and and as a molten looking glass?
19: For For we cannot order our speech our speech by reason of darkness.
20: If a man speak, surely he shall be swallowed up.
21: But the wind passeth, and cleanseth them.
22: With God is is terrible majesty.
23: And in plenty of justice: he will not afflict.
24: He respecteth not any that are that are wise of heart.
